steps.title: Substitute the input
steps.content: The function is $f(x)=x^3+14$. To find $f(3)$, replace every $x$ with $3$: $$f(3)=3^3+14.$$

steps.title: Apply the exponent first
steps.content: $3^3=27$, so $$f(3)=27+14=41.$$ Do not read $x^3$ as $3x$; that mistake would give $23$ instead.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The computed value is $41$, which matches C. The nearby answers are traps from arithmetic or exponent mistakes, not alternate valid function values.
